hero.title: 12 Days in Sri Lanka.
hero.titleAccent: North, Central and South — the Whole Island.
hero.intro: Twelve days is the only way to see Sri Lanka fully. Jaffna and Nainativu Island in the north, the ancient UNESCO sites at Anuradhapura, Sigiriya and Polonnaruwa through the centre, Kandy and the tea hills, and Yala wildlife safari with the south coast. The whole island, done properly.

breadcrumb.label: 12 Days Best of Sri Lanka: Central + North + South

excludes: Entrance Fees to temples and sites

overview.heading: 12 Days Best of Sri Lanka: Central + North + South: The Full Picture
body: Sri Lanka rewards travellers who move through it at the right pace — not rushing, not lingering too long. This 12 Days / 11 Nights itinerary is designed to cover Jaffna, Anuradhapura, Sigiriya, Kandy, Nuwara Eliya, Yala, South Coast with the right balance of time at each stop.

Every Dhesu Sri Lanka package runs as a 100% private tour. Your own vehicle, your own English-speaking guide, your own schedule. No coaches, no group waiting times, no missed sights because the group voted differently. The itinerary below is a guide — it can be adjusted to your interests and pace.

highlights.heading: Why Malaysian Travellers Love This Sri Lanka Package

items.title: Sigiriya Rock Fortress
items.body: Rising 200 metres from the Sri Lankan jungle, Sigiriya is the island's single most spectacular site. A 5th-century king built his palace on top, complete with frescoes, mirror walls and water gardens. The climb takes about 45 minutes and the views from the summit stretch to the horizon.
items.imageAlt: Sigiriya Rock Fortress Sri Lanka aerial view

items.title: Temple of the Sacred Tooth Relic, Kandy
items.body: Sri Lanka's holiest Buddhist shrine, set beside Kandy Lake in the last royal capital. The evening puja ceremony with drums and incense is one of the most atmospheric experiences in the country.
items.imageAlt: Temple of the Sacred Tooth Relic Kandy Sri Lanka

items.title: Nuwara Eliya & the Tea Hills
items.body: The colonial hill station at 1,868 metres, surrounded by tea estates that produce Ceylon's finest highland teas. The cool air, rose gardens and scenic railway views make this the most surprising corner of the island.
items.imageAlt: Nuwara Eliya tea plantations Sri Lanka hill country

days.title: Arrive Colombo
days.description: Arrive at Bandaranaike International Airport. Private transfer to Colombo hotel.

days.title: Colombo City & Drive to Anuradhapura
days.description: Morning Colombo city tour. Afternoon drive north to Anuradhapura — Sri Lanka's first ancient capital.

days.title: Anuradhapura Ancient Capital
days.description: Full-day Anuradhapura: Sacred Bodhi Tree (2,300 years old), Ruvanvelisaya Dagoba, Thuparama Dagoba and the Abhayagiri Monastery ruins — a complete ancient civilisation.

days.title: Drive to Jaffna
days.description: Drive north through the Vavuniya plains and the Elephant Pass causeway to Jaffna. The landscape changes completely — palmyra palms, lagoons, and Tamil architecture.

days.title: Jaffna City & Nainativu Island
days.description: Morning Jaffna Fort, Nallur Kandaswamy Kovil and the local market. Afternoon boat to Nainativu Island for the Nagapooshani Amman and Nagadeepa Buddhist temples.

days.title: Jaffna to Sigiriya
days.description: Drive south from Jaffna. Stop at Dambulla Cave Temple. Overnight near Sigiriya in the Cultural Triangle.

days.title: Sigiriya & Polonnaruwa
days.description: Early morning Sigiriya Rock Fortress climb. Afternoon Polonnaruwa — the second ancient capital with extraordinary carved stone ruins.

days.title: Kandy — Heart of Sri Lanka
days.description: Drive to Kandy via Matale. Temple of the Sacred Tooth Relic, Peradeniya Botanical Gardens, Kandy Lake and evening cultural show.

days.title: Tea Country — Nuwara Eliya
days.description: Scenic mountain drive to Nuwara Eliya. Tea estate and factory tour. Horton Plains National Park walk with World's End views.

days.title: Nuwara Eliya to Yala
days.description: Morning Ella (Nine Arch Bridge and Ravana Falls). Afternoon drive to Yala National Park via Kataragama Temple.

days.title: Yala Wildlife Safari & South Coast
days.description: Dawn and afternoon Yala jeep safaris — maximum wildlife exposure including leopard and elephant sightings. Evening drive to the south coast beach.

days.title: South Coast & Depart Colombo
days.description: Morning at the south coast beach. Southern Expressway drive to Colombo for your international flight home.

hotelOptions.heading: Choose Your Comfort Level
hotelOptions.subheading: Hotels selected for location and views. Sri Lanka's mid-range hotels are excellent value — 4-star properties with pool and mountain or ocean views are the sweet spot for most Malaysian travellers.

whyBook.heading: Sri Lanka Made Right, Since 1988
whyBook.body: Sri Lanka is deceptively complex — the traffic in Colombo, the timing at Sigiriya, the Yala driver who knows the leopard territories. We have been arranging these journeys since 1988 and the details are in our DNA.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We guide you through Sri Lanka ETA documentation with every booking.

points.title: Private Tours, Daily Departures
points.body: No minimum group to wait for. Your trip departs on your dates in your own private vehicle.

points.title: A Real Team to Call
points.body: Reachable throughout your trip from Colombo to Jaffna to Yala. If anything changes, we know what to do.

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing
points.body: Travelling with family or a group reduces the per-person cost. Ask us for a tailored quote.

sections.heading: 12 Days Best of Sri Lanka: Central + North + South from Malaysia
sections.body:
This 12 days Sri Lanka package covers Jaffna, Anuradhapura, Sigiriya, Kandy, Nuwara Eliya, Yala, South Coast. Starting from RM4,528 per person on twin sharing, it runs as a 100% private tour with an English-speaking guide, private vehicle and airport transfers included throughout. International flights are not included — most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur to Colombo in around 3.5 hours.

Dhesu has been arranging Sri Lanka tours for Malaysian travellers since 1988. Our team knows which hotels face the lake in Kandy, when to arrive at Sigiriya for the fewest crowds, and the jeep drivers in Yala who know the leopard territories. This is not a package put together from a website — it is 35 years of ground-level knowledge.

sections.heading: Sri Lanka Visa for Malaysian Travellers
sections.body: Malaysian passport holders need an Electronic Travel Authorisation (ETA) to enter Sri Lanka. The ETA is applied for online at eta.gov.lk before travel and costs USD 35 for a 30-day single-entry tourist visa. Processing is usually approved within 24 hours. Dhesu provides full ETA application guidance with every Sri Lanka booking, including the correct form fields and photograph requirements.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for Sri Lanka?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders must apply for a Sri Lanka Electronic Travel Authorisation (ETA) before travel. The ETA costs USD 35 for a 30-day single-entry tourist visa and can be applied for online at eta.gov.lk. Dhesu provides full documentation guidance with every booking.

items.question: Which airport does this package use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package covering hotels, private vehicle with guide, tours and airport transfers. International flights are not included. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur (KLIA) to Colombo Bandaranaike International Airport (CMB) on SriLankan Airlines or AirAsia. We recommend suitable flights on request.

items.question: What is the best time to visit Sri Lanka from Malaysia?
items.answer: November to April is the best time to visit the west coast, Kandy, the Cultural Triangle and Yala. The east coast and Jaffna are best May to September when the southwest monsoon arrives. Sri Lanka's hill country is pleasant year-round, with afternoon showers adding to the atmosphere. We recommend October to March for most first-time visitors.

items.question: Is this a private tour or a group tour?
items.answer: All Dhesu Sri Lanka packages run as 100% private tours. You have your own vehicle and guide for the entire duration. There are no shared coaches, no waiting for other guests, and the itinerary can be adjusted to your pace.

items.question: Are halal food options available in Sri Lanka?
items.answer: Sri Lanka has a significant Muslim community (about 10% of the population) and halal food is available throughout the country, especially in Colombo, Kandy and Galle. Most hotels accommodate halal dietary requirements with advance notice. Our Muslim-friendly packages specifically arrange halal meals at each meal stop.
